channel.title: Waf on OpenSwap Guide
channel.description: Recent content in Waf on OpenSwap Guide

item.title: BunkerWeb vs ModSecurity vs CrowdSec: Best Self-Hosted WAF Guide 2026
item.description: Protecting web applications from attacks like SQL injection, cross-site scripting (XSS), and bot abuse is essential — whether you run a single blog or a multi-tenant platform. Commercial WAFs (Cloudflare, AWS WAF) cost money and route your traffic through third-party infrastructure. Self-hosted open-source alternatives give you full control over your security posture without the per-request pricing.

item.title: Best Self-Hosted WAF & Bot Protection: ModSecurity vs Coraza vs CrowdSec 2026
item.description:
Complete Guide to Self-Hosted WAF & Bot Protection 2026

Every public-facing web application is under constant attack. SQL injection, cross-site scripting, credential stuffing, and automated bot scraping happen around the clock. A cloud-hosted Web Application Firewall (WAF) like Cloudflare or AWS WAF can cost anywhere from $20 to hundreds of dollars per month — and your traffic data flows through a third party.
